Title: [AdsBrain DRI] <call for help about Singularity TaskCanceledException error>
Summary:
Please check our
doc
first before creating ICM ticket.
Category   (ABI/FlexABI/FastRefresh/BatchScore/GPTEmbedding/ABO)
BatchScore
Aether job link (if exists)
aether://experiments/88657852-cba8-4c29-a6f1-c624a46a05bf?nodeIdPath=4ff500eb%2Cf8cf85e2
AdsBrain Aether subgraph version
Singularity job URL (if using ITP/Singularity)
DLIS offline job id (if using DLIS)
AML job link (if BatchScore/GPTEmbedding)
Description
This task has been running normally in the past (scheduled once every two days), but the last two runs reported an error "Error submitting job to GlobalJobDispatcher server: System.Threading.Tasks.TaskCanceledException: A task was canceled.
" and there is no Singularity job URL. Could you help me check it? Thank you very much.
Investigation: AETHER\AEther
AI_KeyLog: [2025-05-30 02:52:23Z] Error submitting job to GlobalJobDispatcher server: System.Threading.Tasks.TaskCanceledException: A task was canceled.
AI_Conclusion: The issue was identified as a transient problem related to MSAL, which should have been mitigated a few days prior.
AI_Solution: The user was advised to try resubmitting the job, as the issue was expected to be resolved.

Title: [AML Pipeline Submission Issue] AML pipeline returns 400 during submission
Summary:
When submitting AML pipeline, it is returning 400 status. Pipeline goes to "draft" mode and when retrieving this draft pipeline, it is giving same error (attached in screenshot).
Previously, this same AML pipeline is working fine. Cloning and submitting same pipeline giving this issue.
Pipeline link:
Authoring - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
workspace:
amlworkspace3oiz66dnocaqy
Detailed error:
{   "error": {     "code": "UserError",     "message": "Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'key')",     "additionalInfo": null,     "details": []   },   "correlation": {     "operation": "aa312433bc6c430aa195faf7f2dc4d64",     "request": "f2dff33c5e517c3b"   } }
Error screenshot:
Investigation: GetGraphDraftV2 throw null exception.Considering referenced component asset has been deleted
AI_KeyLog: Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'key')
AI_Conclusion: The error is caused by missing components in the pipeline graph, which cannot be found in the backend.
AI_Solution: Check whether the components in the graph still exist and ensure that all required components are available.

Title: [AML Pipelines] - [CSS][S500][SR#2503240030006760][Title - Logs stopped and pipeline running]
Summary:
Please describe the issues you are experiencing with AML Pipelines or Execution Service:
Problem description
Ongoing issue where customer is reporting that the pipeline executes each day at 11pm IST.
This pipeline essentially executes requests to Az Open AI and retrieves and consolidates the data at the latest step.
The issue is that at certain point the logs stop and customer is unable to understand whether the data consol job step is ongoing or if there is any issue, and if there is an issue then where so they can fine tune it to avoid this.
The execution is heavy, it normally takes 14 hours, on Fridays night takes almost 2 days to exec due to weekly data.
Although it executes successfully most of the times, customer would like to be able to continue seeing the logs so they understand what is being processed and when it stops.
The question is, on our backend is it stopped or is successful but its taking time to report back to UI?

Title: [AML Pipelines] - [CSS][S500][SR#2506160050004047|2506160050004006][Title - Supportability of Pipeline jobs on Hub/Project]
Summary:
- ResourceUri: /subscriptions/1fe5d9d8-7eb5-48c7-a447-dd9683a5cc1f/resourceGroups/rg-skh-0001-dev-ext002-ml/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/aipr-skh-0001-dev-ext002
Region: FranceCentral
Workspace Kind: Project
Network Isolation: Managed network/Allow only Approved.
ACR: Network access disabled / Admin User Disabled /PE from managed vnet of the Hub already created.
Issue:
While customer has been building and running pipelines on Hub/Projects since 2 months, they have started to facing issues with image build task and entire pipeline functionality on serverless computes on AML project resource.
The issue is experienced over almost all of their Hub/projects resources now except for a few (1 confirmed by myself) that are still working for
Same pipeline code base
.
From what I understand, when ACR and/or other default dependent resources
restrict public network access,
we typically guide customers to configure a dedicated image build compute
cluster:
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/machine-learning/how-to-managed-network?view=azureml-api-2&tabs=new-workspace&pivots=cli#configure-image-builds
Since Hubs/Projects are essentially AML workspaces, I would assume this
guidance applies here as well.
Am I correct in this assumption, or is there something specific to
Hubs/Projects that I might be missing?
However, given that Hub/Project doesn’t support compute clusters, I’m concerned this
limitation could block the customer from proceeding with their pipeline jobs.
There was long discussions are the supportability status of pipeline jobs on Hubs/Project, that ended up by advising that pipeline jobs are supposed to function if there were to run on Serverless computes. ==> This was true and running as expected for 2 months until it started to break this week.
Kindly note that there are no logs on customer side (Output+logs has not indication to issue neither on step nor on image build run).

Title: [AML Pipelines] - Data Import jobs fail when selecting user datastore with Managed Identity Auth
Summary:
If a user tries to run a Data Import job in AML Studio and select their own datastore (default or one they created) that uses Managed Identity auth instead of Access Keys, the Import Job will fail with an error like the one below. Given that customers are being encouraged to use Managed Identity auth as the default for their default and created datastores, we should try to support this case so that customers don't have to compromise their current setups. This issue is present in all clouds.
Data Factory run failed with UserError, Error response:[{
"errorCode": "2200",
"message": "ErrorCode=AzureBlobFailedToCreateContainer,'Type=Microsoft.DataTransfer.Common.Shared.HybridDeliveryException,Message=Unable to create Azure Blob container. Endpoint: 'https://jloefflerws0415442395405.blob.core.windows.net/', Container Name: 'azureml-blobstore-c1334791-29a5-40d8-a0c0-5ae09f7abb78'.,Source=Microsoft.DataTransfer.ClientLibrary,''Type=Microsoft.WindowsAzure.Storage.StorageException,Message=The remote server returned an error: (403) Forbidden.,Source=Microsoft.WindowsAzure.Storage,StorageExtendedMessage=RequestId:d5538a0d-801e-0017-7ae5-ba3bb0000000 Time:Thu, 01 May 2025 22:07:28 GMT,,''Type=System.Net.WebException,Message=The remote server returned an error: (403) Forbidden.,Source=System,'",
"failureType": "UserError",
"target": "CopyActivity0",
"details": []
}]
Investigation: The job is using a Hobo ADF to copy data from sql to storage. Failed with permission error. Hobo ADF has Contributor role only, not Storage Blob Contributor.Transferred to common hobo team
AI_KeyLog: ErrorCode=AzureBlobFailedToCreateContainer, 'Type=Microsoft.DataTransfer.Common.Shared.HybridDeliveryException, Message=Unable to create Azure Blob container. Endpoint: 'https://jloefflerws0415442395405.blob.core.windows.net/', Container Name: 'azureml-blobstore-c1334791-29a5-40d8-a0c0-5ae09f7abb78'. The remote server returned an error: (403) Forbidden.
AI_Conclusion: The job failed due to insufficient permissions for the ADF to create a blob container, requiring the Blob Contributor role.
AI_Solution: Check and ensure that the ADF has the Storage Data Contributor Role permission to the storage account.

Title: [Copilot Tuning] Not able to use custom_model in TDP environment
Summary:
We are testing out a new use case where we use a model in the `azureml` AML registry in one of our pipelines. We have been able to validate this in an eyes-on environment, but encounter an issue in the TDP environment.
TDP_job: Link to failing job in TDP env:
Tenant Copilot Data Preparation - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
eyes_on_job: Link to job in an eyes-on environment (NOTE: this on also failed because I used test data. However, this job passed the point of failure of the TDP job):
Pipeline-Created-on-06-12-2025 - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
Notice that in the TDP_job, the pipeline fails before any logs are generated in the first component. In the eyes_on_job, the pipeline starts generating logs and the component fails because it's dealing with some random data. The random data was used to see if it could get passed the point of failure in the TDP_job, as that is all that was needed.
I ran some additional experiments in the TDP environment to rule some things out:
1. I tried a different TDP environment. Same issue.
2. I tried using a different custom_model. Same issue.
3. I tried using a different custom_model from a different AML registry. Same issue.
This is the component we are trying to run:
sargy_with_labels - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
This component is currently using this custom model:
Phi-3.5-mini-instruct: 6 - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
N/A
Investigation: Tracked with work item. Task 4332533: [DCM] Nodeprocessor issue to check data availability for registry data in compliant workspace
AI_KeyLog: You do not have permissions to use the access key to list data. The client guvijaya_jit@prdtrs01.prod.outlook.com does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/listKeys/action.
AI_Conclusion: The issue is caused by the 'Data Availability Precheck' in compliant workspaces, which fails when trying to use models from the registry due to missing input data.
AI_Solution: Temporary workaround is to manually copy the model into the compliant workspace or run it in a non-compliant workspace.

Title: [CSS] S500 | Customer is unable to use Vector Search due to outbound connection.
Summary:
The CX is attempting to run vector search in AI Foundry's prompt flow in an AOAO environment. They are in a highly restricted environment and can only add rules to the outbound if it is our public documentation. This CX has added all the rules in our documentation but when they use the preview feature, "Vector Search" as an option. It fails as it makes a call to openaipublic.blob... which appears to be a public link to get model information.
requests.exceptions.ConnectTimeout: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='openaipublic.blob.core.windows.net', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /encodings/cl100k_base.tiktoken (Caused by ConnectTimeoutError(<urllib3.connection.HTTPSConnection object at 0x7f684f42a650>, 'Connection to openaipublic.blob.core.windows.net timed out. (connect timeout=None)'))
I am creating this ICM to see if the above is a required outbound rule? If so, is it possible update our documentation through a work item?
If not, then what is the recommended workaround for the above error?
Investigation: Cx facing issue when using vector search in a restricted AOAO environment. Vector Index team updated the package to fix this problem. [TODO] Cx unable to access PYPI in environment and need ETA for runtime release.
AI_KeyLog: requests.exceptions.ConnectTimeout: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='openaipublic.blob.core.windows.net', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /encodings/cl100k_base.tiktoken (Caused by ConnectTimeoutError)
AI_Conclusion: The issue is related to the customer's restricted environment not allowing outbound access to required resources, specifically the openaipublic blob for model information.
AI_Solution: Two options provided: Option 1 - If the customer can access pypi.org, add 'azureml-rag==0.2.39.2' to requirements.txt. Option 2 - Wait for the new version of promptflow runtime image that includes the fix.

Title: [css][2505020030002262][PromptFlowPortal] Prompt Flow Consistency Issue with GPT-4o
Summary:
I'm reaching out to check if there have been any recent updates or changes to Prompt Flow, particularly related to its behavior with GPT-4o. We’ve observed a case where the same prompt previously returned a valid response, but now fails to do so under identical conditions. This is impacting our testing, and we’d like to understand if there’s been any backend or model-related change that could explain the shift in behavior.
summary below:
Using the same GPT-4o model with the RAG (prompt flow) architecture
The same question was successfully answered a few days ago.
Today, asking the same question no longer returns a valid response.
Adjusting the temperature to 0.5 resulted in a response, but the content was incorrect.
Switching to GPT-4o Mini produced a correct response.
can answer questions change to 0.5
can't answer question:
Investigation: Prompt Flow Consistency Issue with GPT-4o. No root cause found.
AI_KeyLog: Inconsistency with the model; bad requests were using the ev3 engine; no changes found for locations Australia East between specified times.
AI_Conclusion: The issue is likely caused by inconsistent behavior of GPT-4o models, not related to PromptFlow SDK. The customer needs to analyze their logs for any changes impacting deployment or prompt behavior.
AI_Solution: Customer to collect logs, try other models, and share final prompt and parameters (though they cannot share due to customer info).
AI_TransferredTo: Azure OpenAI Service/AOAI API and Model Functionality, then back to Project Vienna Services/PromptFlow Cloud Control Plane

Title: [CSS][Prem - CEGID SAS][SR#2503060050001384 ][Title - DatastoreSDK “workspaceworkingdirectory with type AzureFile is not supported” Error]
Summary:
Please check the following links to see it's a known issue:
For office jobs, look up in
Commonly Hit User Errors and Platform Limitations
. If you are not sure which ICM team to fire ticket, check
this document
.

DataDrift hard limitations

repla
ce "TOPIC" in title
with one of below strings, and
fill in the region in title.
SubstrateML
Only for Office Rufous jobs
DatasetSDK
SDK problems only, switch to DataService team if it's about service
DatastoreSDK
SDK problems only, switch to DataService team if it's about service
DataDrift
Also known as "Dataset Monitor"
Issue Description:
What happened? (description or screenshot)
The customer is encountering an error in Azure Machine Learning Prompt Flow runs:
"Data store workspaceworkingdirectory with type AzureFile is not supported."
This occurs consistently (100% failure) when running multiple flows that attempt to use the “workspaceworkingdirectory” datastore.
Context so far:
The AML Workspace name is "amlctudev001" (Resource URI: /subscriptions/6b18b854-398b-4dc7-9828-c492ac221f23/resourceGroups/ctu-dev-fc-rg/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/amlctudev001).
Customer originally deployed a Prompt Flow referencing an Azure OpenAI resource in Sweden Central, while the AML workspace itself appears to be in France Central.
The error has persisted since 2025-03-06T10:02:00.
Multiple reproduction attempts in internal test labs were unsuccessful.
The user triggers deployments via the AML UI.
No GPU-based deployments; model type/size not specified.
How is the impact? Does it blocking you?
The user states this blocks them from successfully running Prompt Flow due to the datastore error.
The customer is frustrated and has paused further troubleshooting until Microsoft provides a conclusive next step.
Error Messages?
"Data store workspaceworkingdirectory with type AzureFile is not supported."
Key information for investigation: (
* is mandatory
)
*
Subscription Id
6b18b854-398b-4dc7-9828-c492ac221f23
a GUID string, can be found in your workspace config.json or logs such as "/subscription/
xxx
/"
Workspace name
amlctudev001
your workspace name, on top left corner of your portal
*
Experiment Id or Pipeline Run Id
Not provided (flows are triggered from built-in “Chat with Wikipedia” sample in Prompt Flow)
could be found on your portal by clicking on "Experiments"
*
Run Id
Not applicable
could be found on your portal by click on a specific run and check it's "Details"
Please Also Note
Sev 3 and 4 are the appropriate settings for issues affecting a single user.
Sev 2 is for region-wide service outage
Sev 1 is for global outage.
Investigation: Promptflow only support workspace default datastore to be AzureBlob. The user changed the default datastore to "workspaceworkingdirectory" of type AzureFile, which is not supported and should cause lots of problems other than promptflow cannot run.
AI_KeyLog: Data store workspaceworkingdirectory with type AzureFile is not supported.
AI_Conclusion: The user changed the default datastore to 'workspaceworkingdirectory' of type AzureFile, which is not supported by PromptFlow, leading to consistent errors.
AI_Solution: The user needs to revert the default datastore to AzureBlob, as PromptFlow only supports this type.
AI_TransferredTo: Project Vienna Services/PromptFlow Cloud Control Plane

Title: [CSS][Prem][SR#2506030030005152| ISSUE IN VIEWING PACKAGES
Summary:
Customer workspace details:
/subscriptions/2ab74a87-593d-4cad-a899-0dc6458494ac/resourceGroups/RG-DESRY-AI-FOUNDRY/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/desri-aifoundry-service
region: eastus
ai foundry name:
desri-aifoundry-service
cx had created ai foundry with private endpoints.
cx trying to view the packages installed in promptflow compute instance
but while checking it he was unable to view them
Cx resource information- Relevant to case
Workspace -/subscriptions/2ab74a87-593d-4cad-a899-0dc6458494ac/resourceGroups/RG-DESRY-AI-FOUNDRY/providers/Microsoft.CognitiveServices/accounts/desri-aifoundry-service
Compute -serverless
Error screenshots- If Any
Repro steps- In case required
i have tried to view the installed packages mentioned in requirements.txt file in promptflow.
I was able to view the packages.
ASK to icm: kindly check why the packages are not visible for cx
Investigation: Cannot view packages. Call to training session timeout. Training session api returned, negotiate to increase timeout. Made a PR to increase timeout. Pull Request 1703208: [PMT][Internal] Update TrainingSession API Timeout to 30s Made change to increase timeout to 30s.
AI_KeyLog: Service invocation timed out. Unable to read data from the transport connection: Operation canceled. Time waited: 00:00:09.9994288
AI_Conclusion: The issue is caused by a timeout when attempting to run a command in the container, indicating a potential performance issue or misconfiguration in the training session service.
AI_Solution: Increase the timeout value for the command execution in the training session.

AI_KeyLog: Resource is unhealthy. Condition: AlertRule1: ErrorPercentage > 2, 3/6 failed buckets. Evaluated value: 3.

Title: [ModelFun] - Control output port does not create control signal properly in dv3batch workspace
Summary:
Our user encountered a weird problem when running the pipeline on dv3batch workspace, the
does cosmos dataset exists
module failed to output correctly, leading downstream dsl.condition module failed, while
the one on wxtcstrain workspace runs correctly
. Can you help investigate? The difference seems to be for the failed run, control signal was output to Cosmos, but for the successful one, it was output to blob storage. But there is no difference in user pipeline code.
Failure in dv3batch:
does cosmos dataset exists
Successful in wxtcstrain:
the one on wxtcstrain workspace runs correctly
Investigation: Wait for the customer's response
AI_KeyLog: The successful one (in workspace wxtcstrain) works because of a service bug from our side. It should not work based on 'Does Cosmos Dataset Exist' definition.
AI_Conclusion: The issue is due to a service bug that allowed the control output to work in the wxtcstrain workspace, which should not have been the case according to the component definition.
AI_Solution: Modify the 'Does Cosmos Dataset Exist' component based on the provided documentation and ensure the component definition is correct.

When using lightning code to do inference, the job shows the ckpt path doesn't exist when I pass ckpt="/azureml/e06be5ff-91f8-46cf-851d-741b7495cc24/output_path/checkpoints/epoch=2-step=5500.ckpt/mp_rank_00_model_states.pt" to download_path_1
error job link:
BGE_Gemma2_9B_Stage1_Standard - Azure Machine Learning
If all settings are correct, when pass ckpt='/azureml/e06be5ff-91f8-46cf-851d-741b7495cc24/output_path/checkpoints/epoch=2-step=5500.ckpt/mp_rank_00_model_states.pt', it should show as below:
correct job link:
BingLLM1.5B_Stage1_Premium - Azure Machine Learning

Please describe your issue in as much detail as possible and attach any screenshots or logs that you feel may be beneficial.

*INCIDENT DETAILS HERE*
Investigation: AISUPERCOMPUTER\Storage
AI_KeyLog: Directory Not Found
AI_Conclusion: The checkpoint file was stored in /tmp, which is periodically cleaned up by the OS, leading to the errors reported.
AI_Solution: Change the checkpoint folder to a persistent path instead of /tmp.

Title: [ModelFun] - KNN failing

Hi,
The KNN inference module keeps failing after running for some time due to different errors. The job previously ran successfully on the exact same setting (different data but same data size and format).
Failed job:
query_index_v0_fbv6vec_search_FY25H1 - Azure Machine Learning
JobAlreadyStoppedOnError: The job stopped on error before this node 138d485e-5c5f-491d-b610-7b2c21df35b2_0 exits. Please check prior errors or master log under sys folder for the cause. Refer to https://aka.ms/prstsg for more troubleshooting guidance.

query_index_v0_fbv6vec_search_FY25H1 - Azure Machine Learning
OSError: [Errno 28] No space left on device
Completed job:
query_index_v0_fbv6vec_search_FY25H1 - Azure Machine Learning
Investigation: Customer error. Provided mitigation step.
AI_KeyLog: JobAlreadyStoppedOnError: The job stopped on error before this node 138d485e-5c5f-491d-b610-7b2c21df35b2_0 exits.
AI_Conclusion: The root cause of the retries is node preemption due to the SLATier being set to Standard, leading to failures in minibatch processing.
AI_Solution: Use Premium tier to avoid preemption or add '--amlbi_skip_preemption_retry True' to PRS job's argument.

Title: [ModelFun] - MLFlowLogger ReadTimeoutError Warning in smile-lightning training
Summary:
During the training process of "smile lightning," I noticed that each call to MLFlowLogger results in two ReadTimeoutError occurrences, with each timeout lasting 120 seconds. While this error doesn't disrupt the training, it leads to a 240-second waste. Are there any ways to resolve this error or reduce the timeout duration?
liger kernel test 2 gpu baseline - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
[2025-05-07 03:29:36,546][urllib3.connectionpool][WARNING] - Retrying (JitteredRetry(total=4, connect=5, read=4, redirect=5, status=5)) after connection broken by 'ReadTimeoutError("HTTPSConnectionPool(host='westus3.api.azureml.ms', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=120)")': /mlflow/v2.0/subscriptions/07c3f5a2-0880-4a37-b678-e29ade7b1036/resourceGroups/singularitywestus3/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/wxtcssingwestus3/api/2.0/preview/mlflow/runs/log-batch-async
Retrying (JitteredRetry(total=4, connect=5, read=4, redirect=5, status=5)) after connection broken by 'ReadTimeoutError("HTTPSConnectionPool(host='westus3.api.azureml.ms', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=120)")': /mlflow/v2.0/subscriptions/07c3f5a2-0880-4a37-b678-e29ade7b1036/resourceGroups/singularitywestus3/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/wxtcssingwestus3/api/2.0/preview/mlflow/runs/log-batch-async
Investigation: Mlflow request timeout.Transfer to ModelRegistryAndMLFlowService team
AI_KeyLog: ReadTimeoutError("HTTPSConnectionPool(host='westus3.api.azureml.ms', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=120)")
AI_Conclusion: The issue was related to resource starvation during the logging process, leading to timeouts.
AI_Solution: User changed client side timeout configuration to 'export MLFLOW_HTTP_REQUEST_TIMEOUT=5', which resolved the issue.

I have a prs job which is constantly hanging and failing for some data splits :
onedcg stamping for 200M desktop/mobile data using row 2 basic high - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
I tried rebasing to master and rerunning the inference multiple times, but it always keeps failing with a timeout error
onedcg stamping for 200M desktop/mobile data using row 2 basic high - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
I also tried increasing number of splits from 100 to 1000, but it keeps failing still with timeout error
onedcg stamping for 200M desktop/mobile data using row 2 basic high, 1000 splits - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio
And if I use train module instead of inference, it just hangs
onedcg stamping for 200M desktop/mobile data using row 2 basic high - Azure AI | Machine Learning Studio

Investigation: Help find the init error, so that transfer back to core search modelfun team
AI_KeyLog: sudo: setrlimit(RLIMIT_STACK): Operation not permitted
AI_Conclusion: The timeout errors were caused by init errors in the entry script, exacerbated by insufficient logging level which hid error details.
AI_Solution: Mitigated by suggesting to rerun the job with logging_level=DEBUG and reducing the number of splits to <5.

Title: [PromptFlowPortal] [CSS][Prem][SR#2505140050000796 ][Title - ai foundry with allow only approved outbound network error]
Summary:
The screenshot of the error you encountered:
How to repro the issue:
Create a generative AI app that uses your own data | Develop generative AI solutions in Azure
runtime type: compute instance
Issue: Customer is unable to run Prompt flow in Azure foundry with Managed network(allow only approved outbound), while same sample prompt flow works fine in another Foundry with Public network enabled.
In ai foundry resource with managed virtual network and 'allow only approved outbound' network setting,
customer created user-defined outbound rules to openai and aisearch resource.
He successfully imported custom data and created index.
Then he created prompt flow 'Multi-Round Q&A on Your Data' from MS gallery and started compute session.
When configuring 'lookup' step in prompt flow with search index and openai text-embedding model, he gets the following error when saving:(See error in attachment)
'Fail to generate: {'error':'Request POST /aml-api/v1.0/retrieve_tool_func_result timeout'}.'
We confirmed that all connected resources including storage account, Open AI service and AI search service, etc. is same for both Azure Foundry resources.
We collected the Network traces for failed deployment and found several 408 errors. Please see the HAR file attached.
- ResourceUri: /subscriptions/19dd865c-ba64-4a74-b6b8-9ed718c63fb3/resourceGroups/rg-be-l-dev-pso-001/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/aifoundry-be-l-dev-we-pso-new-006
Cx is able to create indexes successfully in same Azure Foundry:
Investigation: Flow without index lookup tool works fine. Transfer to RAG team.
AI_KeyLog: 'Fail to generate: {'error':'Request POST /aml-api/v1.0/retrieve_tool_func_result timeout'}.
AI_Conclusion: The root cause is that the OpenAI tiktoken library cannot access the OpenAI public blob to download necessary metadata files due to the managed network setup, which lacks the required outbound rule.
AI_Solution: Add an approved outbound rule for the OpenAI public blob: https://openaipublic.blob.core.windows.net. A fix is being worked on but may take some time.
AI_TransferredTo: Project Vienna Services/PromptFlow Cloud Control Plane

Investigation: Transferred to  Project Vienna Services/Networking.
AI_KeyLog: Access to this resource is denied. source_ip_not_in_allowed_public_ip_ranges
AI_Conclusion: The issue is related to the IPAllowList not syncing properly with the inferencing service, leading to access denied errors due to client IPs not being recognized.
AI_Solution: Upgrade the runtime version and ensure the IPAllowList is updated correctly; a fix is being implemented to notify IDP about changes to the IPAllowList.
AI_TransferredTo: Project Vienna Services/Networking, Project Vienna Services/Infrastructure

Title: [PromptFlowPortal] [CSS][S500/Prem/Pro][SR#2504010050002875] [Title - unable to run evaluate flow in ai foundry]
Summary:
The Customer is unable to run the evaluation flow in AI Foundry.
While evaluating the prompt flow, the evaluation is automatically being canceled with no error logs.
I have reproduced the issue and getting the same issue as of the customer.
The screenshot of the error cx encountered:
When we reproduced, we saw:
How to repro the issue:
I am attaching the prompt flow and the evaluation data in the attachments. Upload the flow as a chat flow and add a GPT-4o model, text embedding 003-large in the connection and add any sample vector index. Search type must be hybrid+vector.
Evaluate the flow with the given evaluation data.
Resource url:
/subscriptions/44295f46-976c-4fdf-8b38-4c22244fa93e/resourceGroups/rg-oresgenai-dev-sdc/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/aih-oresgenai-dev-sdc
runtime type: compute instance
runtime base image version: Default
client request id: NA
Please attach the logs in the Attachments: Logs show empty
Investigation: User error in dependent run terminated unexpected.
AI_KeyLog: BulkTest run evaluate_f9dae9a1-1995-4c98-968d-430f45c7e775 has a dependent run 9dfa3857-4377-4d54-9cdf-38ea327a410e terminated unexpectedly. Mark it as canceled.
AI_Conclusion: The evaluation flow was canceled due to a dependent run terminating unexpectedly.
AI_Solution: Check the status of the dependent run in the 'Jobs' tab to resolve the issue.

Title: [PromptFlowPortal] [CSS][S500][SR#2504300050002874][AI Foundry can't use o3-mini for AI Quality (NLP) evaluations]
Summary:
Issue Description:
customer is trying to create the Evaluation from ai foundry using o3 mini model.
They are able to create the evaluation however they are encountering the issue
in data section of it where the query status is getting failed.
when we open that query it redirects to prompt flow where the issue is
encountered with error code
Execution failure in
'generate_answer_if_not_exist': (BadRequestError) Error code: 400 - {'error':
{'message': "Unsupported parameter: 'max_tokens' is not supported with
this model. Use 'max_completion_tokens' instead.", 'type': 'invalid_request_error',
'param': 'max_tokens', 'code': 'unsupported_parameter'}}
as there is no option there is to opt for max_completion_token(for o3 mini
model)
• Expect Result:It
should work well with each model
• Current Result::It
is impacting customer query status (It is bug in UI while creation of Evaluation, parameter is not alterable for reasoning series model)
• Issue happened
Timestamp:you can take current time
• Azure
Subscription:d2aede04-e1b0-4db8-ae0c-6e2c65cf0004
• TA/SEE Reviewer:
kaustav
• Azure Resource
URI:/subscriptions/d2aede04-e1b0-4db8-ae0c-6e2c65cf0004/resourceGroups/rg-mg-nonprd-t1-sdc-aifoundry-01/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/oai-mg-nonprd-t1-sdc-aif-poc-01
Repro
:
1.evaluation is created for o3 mini model.
2.
3.once we click on fail status, it will redirect to prompt flow.
where it reflects for API version error.
4.after giving the correct API supported via connection tab in machine learning workspace, this error got mitigated. However we are still getting the max token error while using o3 mini model.
now the error encountered is unsupported parameter.
as O series model uses completion token parameter instead of max response token
Azure OpenAI reasoning models - o3-mini, o1, o1-mini - Azure OpenAI | Microsoft Learn
>>there is no option to change parameter for max response if we are using reasoning model.
Investigation: Promptflow runtime issue. Contract and api version issue.transfer to SDK team
AI_KeyLog: Execution failure in 'generate_answer_if_not_exist': (BadRequestError) Error code: 400 - {'error': {'message': "Unsupported parameter: 'max_tokens' is not supported with this model. Use 'max_completion_tokens' instead.", 'type': 'invalid_request_error', 'param': 'max_tokens', 'code': 'unsupported_parameter'}}
AI_Conclusion: The issue is due to the o3 mini model not supporting the 'max_tokens' parameter, which is causing the evaluation creation to fail. Reasoning models are not currently supported at the platform layer.
AI_Solution: The error was mitigated by providing the correct API supported via the connection tab in the machine learning workspace, but the max token error persists. A task was created to drop all reasoning models from the deployment dropdown.

Title: [southcentralus] QuotaService API availability dropped and is unhealthy
AI_KeyLog: Resource is unhealthy. Condition: AlertRule1: ErrorPercentage > 1, 3/6 failed buckets. Evaluated value: 3.
AI_Conclusion: The QuotaService API availability dropped and is unhealthy due to high error percentage.
AI_Solution: Incident is mitigated by the monitor QuotaService API Availability; further investigation into the watchdog reports is needed.

Summary:

We have expiration policy on the AML blob storage for Intermediate data created to save storage. But it leads to issues with components reuse. AML consider the component still valid for the reuse but the outputs are no longer present in the blob which leads to the next step failing.
Example of the experiment
normalize_ranker - Azure Machine Learning
Is there a mechanism to exclude job from reuse?
I've tried deleting the metadata of the job via different methods but  gettting different errors when trying different version of API
az resource delete --ids "/subscriptions/0bda0759-4545-499e-8460-98f94f96ec48/resourceGroups/image-relevance-rg/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/image-relevance-ws/jobs/898be9e3-1d76-40f2-9ec5-5ddc6012249d" --api-version 2022-05-01 --verbose
Configured default 'Singularity_EUS' for arg resource_group_name
(UserError) A job was found, but it is not supported in this API version and cannot be accessed.
Code: UserError
Message: A job was found, but it is not supported in this API version and cannot be accessed.
az rest --method delete --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/0bda0759-4545-499e-8460-98f94f96ec48/resourceGroups/image-relevance-rg/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/image-relevance-ws/jobs/898be9e3-1d76-40f2-9ec5-5ddc6012249d?api-version=2023-04-01-preview"
A job was found, but it is not supported in this API version and cannot be accessed.({
"error": {
"code": "UserError",
"message": "A job was found, but it is not supported in this API version and cannot be accessed.",
az rest --method delete --url "https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/0bda0759-4545-499e-8460-98f94f96ec48/resourceGroups/image-relevance-rg/providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/image-relevance-ws/experiments/a3e35fef-892a-4b1b-b3eb-e7257ab91ecf/runs/898be9e3-1d76-40f2-9ec5-5ddc6012249d?api-version=2020-08-01"
Not Found
What is the correct mechanism to invalidate run after some period of time to prevent reuse of the jobs with data no longer present?
Investigation: Managed data service set unexpected expiry time.
AI_KeyLog: (UserError) A job was found, but it is not supported in this API version and cannot be accessed.
AI_Conclusion: The Managed Data Service (MDS) does not know when the data is deleted from Blob storage, leading to components being reused despite the data being absent.
AI_Solution: Use the 'Update Dataset' API to set dataset expiry; however, MDS cannot precisely know when to set the dataset as expired due to lack of information from Blob storage.

Investigation: PROJECTVIENNASERVICES\TrainingExecutionService
AI_KeyLog: The total jobs submitted within the same time window are 1234 jobs. Triggered jobs are 1063, while submitted jobs are 1234, which leaves us with 171 extra jobs.
AI_Conclusion: The unexpected job submissions were caused by overlapping schedules and misconfigurations in the job triggers, leading to excessive job submissions and increased costs due to scaling the cluster.
AI_Solution: The customer mitigated the issue by deleting jobs and scaling out the cluster. Further investigation into the job scheduling configuration is recommended.

Incident-632928522 Details - IcM

Investigation: AISUPERCOMPUTER\Storage
AI_KeyLog: UserError: AzureMLCompute job failed : Lifecycler got aggregated errors from capabilities and execution
AI_Conclusion: The issue was caused by the input directory being cleaned up while the upload was attempted, leading to a stream not found error. Changing the output mode to 'mount' resolved the issue.
AI_Solution: Change the output mode from 'upload' to 'mount'.

Title: Pipeline jobs fail even though the child job runs complete successfully

AI_Solution: Investigate the pipeline job team to ensure the uber job does not fail if the child run completes successfully. Monitor the ManagedBatchInference - Job Failure Rate.

AI_KeyLog: [2025-05-13 22:58:45Z] APServiceClient Submit APCloud job to: https://Scopecloud.prod.aetherclouds.ms/scope/
AI_Conclusion: The root cause appears to be related to the pre-submission compilation pass, which is causing delays in job submissions. The issue may be mitigated by removing this compilation pass in the future.
AI_Solution: Consider adding the command-line parameter -off EnableSubmitPreCompilation to eliminate the pre-submission compilation pass. The issue seems to have been mitigated for now.
